reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures "find all functions F: ℝ\{0,1} → ℝ satisfying F(x) + F((x-1)/x) = 1+x". The set S = univ \ {0, 1} correctly represents ℝ\{0,1}, and the functional equation P is stated verbatim on S. Although the Lean encoding uses total functions ℝ → ℝ (which is standard), the uniqueness claim is stated as agreement on S (∀ x ∈ S, f x = F x), which correctly corresponds to uniqueness on the intended domain ℝ\{0,1}. Both directions of "find all" are present: every F in the solution set satisfies the equation on S, and every f satisfying the equation agrees with some F in the solution set on S. Since the solution set is a singleton, this correctly says the solution on S is uniquely determined.
